name: browser-discovery
description: Browser automation for documentation discovery. Use when curl fails on JS-rendered sites, when detecting available browser tools, or when configuring browser-based documentation collection.
capabilities: Detects when browser automation is required versus static fetching
Identifies available browser tools (Antigravity, Cursor, Playwright)
Provides decision tree for selecting the right browser tool
Returns structured data with discovered pages and navigation structure
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: Pure markdown documentation skill. Contains only guidance on browser automation workflows with no executable code. All shell commands shown are documentation examples, not actual execution. No network calls, file writes, or command execution capabilities.
valueStatement: Static tools like curl fail on JavaScript-rendered sites. This skill provides a decision tree for detecting when browser automation is needed and selects the best available browser tool for documentation discovery.
userTitle: Detect browser tools for documentation discovery
limitations: Does not contain browser automation code itself - only guidance
Requires external browser tools to be installed and configured
Cannot execute JavaScript or navigate pages directly
Depends on Chrome debugging port or IDE-specific tools

useCases.title: Scrape JS-rendered docs
useCases.description: Extract documentation from modern sites that use React, Next.js, or Vue frameworks.

useCases.title: Automate browser decisions
useCases.description: Help AI assistants determine when browser automation is needed and which tool to use.

useCases.title: Build doc site maps
useCases.description: Automatically discover and catalog all pages in a documentation site.

promptTemplates.title: Basic discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Use browser-discovery to check if curl works on the target site. If browser automation is needed, identify the best available tool and extract the documentation pages.

promptTemplates.title: Full site crawl
promptTemplates.prompt: Discover all documentation pages on https://example.dev. Start with browser-detection to check if curl works, then use the appropriate browser tool.

promptTemplates.title: Fallback handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Check for available browser automation tools. If none are available, provide setup instructions for Chrome debugging or suggest alternatives.

promptTemplates.title: Production workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pipeline that tries curl first, detects browser tools, uses Playwright wrapper for JS-rendered sites, and returns structured JSON.

bestPractices: Always try curl first before invoking browser automation - it is faster and cheaper
Use accessibility snapshots instead of screenshots for link extraction
Close browser sessions when done to prevent resource leaks
antiPatterns: Using browser automation when curl would return complete content
Skipping tool detection and assuming a specific browser tool exists
Taking screenshots when structured accessibility data would be more useful

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Claude, Codex, and Claude Code each have different browser capabilities available.

faq.question: What are the limits of browser automation?
faq.answer: Browser automation is slower and more resource-intensive than curl. External tools are required.

faq.question: How does this integrate with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Claude Code can launch Chrome with debugging port 9222, then use the Playwright MCP wrapper.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using browser tools?
faq.answer: Browser sessions run in isolated profiles. Each task gets fresh state by default.

faq.question: Why does curl fail on some sites?
faq.answer: Modern sites use JavaScript frameworks like React and Vue to render content dynamically.

faq.question: How is this different from regular web scraping?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on documentation discovery with structured output and accessibility trees.
